title: Exercises: Error Handling in Hybrid Quantum-Classical Systems

exercises.title: Implement a Configurable Retry Executor
exercises.description: Create a flexible retry executor that supports multiple retry strategies and provides detailed execution metrics.
tasks: Implement the RetryStrategy abstract base class with calculate_delay and should_retry methods
Create ExponentialBackoffStrategy with configurable base, initial delay, max delay, and jitter
Create LinearBackoffStrategy as an alternative strategy
Implement RetryExecutor that uses any strategy and tracks metrics
Add support for callback functions to monitor retry progress
exercises.expectedOutput: A flexible retry executor that supports multiple strategies and tracks detailed metrics
hints: Use getattr(error, 'retryable', True) to check if custom errors are retryable
Remember to cap the delay at max_delay before adding jitter
Track errors by type using the exception class name
Call callbacks before sleeping, not after

testCases.expectedBehavior: Returns delay approximately equal to initial_delay * base^2

testCases.input: Operation that fails once then succeeds

exercises.title: Build a Graceful Degradation Manager
exercises.description: Implement a system that automatically adjusts service capabilities based on health metrics and error rates.
tasks: Define degradation levels with different capability sets
Implement health metric tracking and composite health score calculation
Create automatic mode transitions based on health thresholds
Add mode transition history for debugging
Implement capability checking and performance factor retrieval
exercises.starterCode:
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Dict, Any
from enum import Enum
import time

class DegradationMode(Enum):
    FULL = "full"
    REDUCED = "reduced"
    FALLBACK = "fallback"
    MINIMAL = "minimal"

@dataclass
class HealthMetrics:
    quantum_availability: float = 1.0  # 0-1
    error_rate: float = 0.0  # 0-1
    latency_factor: float = 1.0  # 1.0 = normal, higher = slower
    queue_depth: int = 0

@dataclass
class DegradationLevel:
    mode: DegradationMode
    capabilities: List[str]
    performance_factor: float
    health_threshold: float  # Minimum health to enter this mode

class GracefulDegradationManager:
    """Manages system degradation based on health metrics."""
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.current_mode = DegradationMode.FULL

exercises.expectedOutput: A manager that automatically adjusts system capabilities based on health metrics
hints: Use ordered iteration through modes from FULL to MINIMAL
Normalize the queue depth to a 0-1 scale for scoring
Store complete metrics snapshot in transition history
Invert latency_factor for scoring (higher latency = lower score)

exercises.title: Create a Classical Fallback System
exercises.description: Build a fallback registry that provides classical alternatives when quantum execution fails.
tasks: Create a FallbackStrategy abstract class with execute and estimate_quality methods
Implement VQEFallback using classical optimization
Implement SamplingFallback using Monte Carlo methods
Build a FallbackRegistry to register and retrieve fallbacks
Add fallback selection based on problem size and accuracy requirements
exercises.expectedOutput: A registry providing classical alternatives with quality estimates
hints: Use exact diagonalization for small Hamiltonians (< 2^10 dimension)
Monte Carlo sampling quality is nearly independent of problem size
Track the method used in the result dictionary
Quality estimates should decrease with problem size for variational methods

exercises.title: Build a Resilient Quantum Executor
exercises.description: Create a comprehensive executor that combines retry logic, graceful degradation, and fallback handling.
tasks: Integrate RetryExecutor, DegradationManager, and FallbackRegistry
Implement execute_with_resilience method that tries quantum then falls back
Add comprehensive result tracking with execution metadata
Handle all error types appropriately
Provide detailed execution reports for debugging
exercises.starterCode:
from typing import Callable, Dict, Any, Optional
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
import time

@dataclass
class ExecutionResult:
    success: bool
    result: Any
    execution_type: str  # 'quantum', 'fallback', 'cached'
    attempts: int
    total_time: float
    errors: List[Dict] = field(default_factory=list)
    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)

class ResilientQuantumExecutor:
    """
    Combines retry, degradation, and fallback for resilient execution.
    """
    
    def __init__(
        self,

        self.degradation = degradation_manager
        self.fallbacks = fallback_registry

exercises.expectedOutput: A comprehensive executor that handles quantum failures gracefully with fallbacks
hints: Check capability availability before attempting quantum execution
Update degradation metrics based on both success and failure types
Include timing information in execution results
